desc: Iridescent blue-black swallowtail. Stray/rare colonist in SoCal — no native Aristolochia. Toxic; model for a Batesian mimicry complex. Established only where pipevine is cultivated.

fm.Upperside: Iridescent blue-black; single row of pale hindwing spots
fm.Underside: Vivid orange submarginal spots on iridescent blue hindwing

desc: Widespread adaptable swallowtail at home in hilltop chaparral and suburban fennel gardens alike.

fm.Upperside: Black with broad yellow band; blue/orange hindwing eyespot
fm.Underside: Yellow spots may be cream or orange-tinged

desc: Dark, short-tailed swallowtail of arid rocky habitats. LA County populations restricted to upper desert canyons.

fm.Upperside: Black with reduced cream bands; short stubby tails
fm.Underside: Blue and orange hindwing spots on dark ground
fm.Habitat: Rocky canyon walls, arid hillsides above 3,500'

desc: Iconic large yellow swallowtail of western riparian corridors. Strong, sailing flight.

fm.Upperside: Bright yellow with bold black tiger stripes; single tail
fm.Underside: Blue and orange marginal hindwing spots

desc: Cream-white swallowtail replacing the Tiger at higher elevations in the San Gabriel Mountains.

fm.Upperside: Cream-white with black tiger stripes; blue/orange marginal spots
fm.Underside: Cream with subdued markings

desc: Largest western swallowtail — two tails per hindwing diagnostic. Rare stray to LA County from desert ranges.

fm.Upperside: Yellow with narrow stripes; 2 tails per hindwing
fm.Underside: Yellow with blue marginal crescents
fm.Habitat: Desert riparian; vagrant to coastal LA

desc: Cabbage white — introduced European butterfly, now the most abundant butterfly in urban LA. Found in every garden, park, and vacant lot. Larvae eat brassicas.

fm.Upperside: White with black forewing tips; 1 spot (♂) or 2 (♀)
fm.Underside: Pale greenish-yellow hindwing

desc: Checkered white — boldly checkered white of weedy disturbed habitats. More heavily marked than Cabbage White. On mustards. Declining in some areas.

fm.Upperside: White with heavy charcoal checkering; ♀ darker
fm.Underside: Olive-green vein markings on hindwing

desc: Delicate, lightly marked white of montane chaparral. Single-brooded spring flier.

fm.Upperside: White with light gray-green vein-end markings
fm.Underside: Hindwing veins yellow-green outlined

desc: Sara orangetip — spring ephemeral with vivid orange wingtips that signal wildflower season has arrived. Males patrol hillsides seeking females. On mustards.

fm.Upperside: White with vivid orange forewing tips (♂); reduced (♀)
fm.Underside: Hindwing marbled green-brown (cryptic)

desc: Small, subtly marbled white. Distinguished from Sara Orangetip by lack of orange tips.

fm.Upperside: White; gray-tipped forewing apex (no orange)
fm.Underside: Finely marbled gray-green; no orange

desc: Orange sulphur — abundant golden-orange butterfly of every alfalfa field, park lawn, and weedy lot in LA. Breeds continuously year-round. Often confused with Cloudless Sulphur.

fm.Upperside: Orange with wide black borders; pink fringes
fm.Underside: Yellow-green with silver cell spot ringed in pink

desc: California state butterfly. Male's forewing pattern resembles a poodle face in silhouette.

fm.Upperside: ♂: yellow with purple-black "dogface" pattern. ♀: plain yellow.
fm.Underside: Yellow to greenish; small pink cell spot

desc: Dainty sulphur — smallest North American pierid. Dainty low flight through weedy lots and desert flats. Yellow with dark forewing tips. Year-round in warm areas.

fm.Upperside: Yellow with black forewing tips
fm.Underside: Winter form darker, heavily dusted

desc: Cloudless sulphur — large bright yellow neotropical butterfly. Colonizes SoCal in warm years, sometimes in spectacular northward migrations. On cassia and senna.

fm.Upperside: ♂: unmarked bright yellow. ♀: paler.
fm.Underside: Yellow-green; reddish cell spot

desc: Purplish copper — small copper butterfly with purple iridescence on males in fresh condition. Wetland edges and moist meadows. Caterpillars on dock and knotweed.

fm.Upperside: ♂: dark with purple sheen/orange band. ♀: orange, heavy spotting.
fm.Underside: Tan with black spots; orange submarginal band

desc: Most widespread NA hairstreak. Subtle gray with orange-and-black hindwing spots.

fm.Upperside: Blue-gray (♂); darker gray (♀)
fm.Underside: Pale gray with white-edged postmedian line; orange spots

desc: Great purple hairstreak — large brilliant iridescent blue hairstreak. Perches high in oak canopy near clumps of mistletoe (larval host). Rarely descends. Stunning when seen.

fm.Upperside: Brilliant iridescent blue; red basal spots
fm.Underside: Black with red-orange basal spots; blue-green highlights

desc: California hairstreak — warm brown hairstreak with orange hindwing crescents. Associated with ceanothus, oak, and mountain mahogany. Males hilltop aggressively.

fm.Upperside: Brown with faint orange marginal spots
fm.Underside: Brown-gray; black spot row edged white; orange crescents

desc: Hedgerow hairstreak — golden-brown hairstreak strongly associated with ceanothus (California lilac). Males hilltop. Spring flier in chaparral and woodland edges.

fm.Upperside: Warm golden-brown
fm.Underside: Golden-brown with postmedian line

desc: Bramble hairstreak — tiny hairstreak with dazzling emerald green underside when fresh. Fades to gray-brown. On buckwheat and lotus in chaparral. Spring flier.

fm.Upperside: Brown (rarely seen)
fm.Underside: Bright emerald green fading to olive; white line

desc: Western pygmy-blue — the smallest butterfly in North America with a wingspan barely reaching ½ inch. Pale brown with blue basal scaling. On saltbush and pigweed in weedy lots.

fm.Upperside: Copper-brown with blue basal wash
fm.Underside: Brown base, white outer; 3 hindwing eyespots
fm.Habitat: Salt marshes, alkali flats, weedy lots

desc: Marine blue — tiny iridescent blue butterfly in constant low flight through urban gardens. Extremely common. Larvae on leadwort and legumes. Year-round in warm areas.

fm.Upperside: Lavender-blue (♂); brown with blue basal (♀)
fm.Underside: Pale with brown/white banding; 2 eyespots
fm.Habitat: Urban gardens, weedy lots, coastal scrub

desc: Echo azure — spring-flying pale blue of riparian corridors and chaparral edges. Among the first blues each year. On ceanothus, buckeye, and other shrubs.

fm.Upperside: Pale silvery-blue with narrow dark margin
fm.Underside: Pale gray with faint dark spots; no orange

desc: Silvery blue — spring-flying blue of open woodland with prominent round black spots on pale blue-gray underside. On lupines and other legumes. Single early-spring brood.

fm.Upperside: Silvery-blue (♂); brown with blue basal (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with single row of prominent round black spots

desc: Square-spotted blue — buckwheat specialist. Distinguished by squarish metallic marginal spots on hindwing underside. Multiple subspecies across SoCal.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂) or brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Orange band with squarish metallic spots
fm.Flight Period: May–Aug; single brood, sync with buckwheat

desc: Western migratory Monarch — IUCN Endangered, USFWS candidate. LA County hosts coastal winter roosts. Population collapsed >95%.

fm.Upperside: Deep orange with black veins; white spots in margins
fm.Underside: Paler; hindwing golden-brown

desc: Queen — the monarch's smaller, darker relative. Desert-edge milkweed-feeder. Rich mahogany brown. Males have black scent patch on hindwing. Less common than Monarch.

fm.Upperside: Rich chestnut-brown; white forewing spots; no black veining
fm.Underside: Chestnut with veins outlined in black

desc: Brilliant orange with extraordinary metallic silver underside. Abundant on Passiflora.

fm.Upperside: Bright orange; 3 white-centered black forewing spots
fm.Underside: Large metallic silver spots (spectacular)

desc: Variegated fritillary — flat-winged tawny fritillary with low sailing flight. Unlike true fritillaries (Argynnis), lacks metallic silver spots on hindwing underside.

fm.Upperside: Tawny with dark zigzag lines; no silver
fm.Underside: Mottled brown/cream; dark median band

desc: World's most widespread butterfly. Spectacular northward irruptions — 2019 triggered billions of migrants through LA.

fm.Upperside: Orange-brown; white forewing patches; irregular black tips
fm.Underside: Complex mosaic; 4–5 small hindwing eyespots

desc: West Coast lady — LA's resident lady butterfly (unlike the migratory Painted Lady). Isolated orange costal bar on forewing diagnostic. On mallows. Year-round.

fm.Upperside: Orange; isolated orange costal bar (diagnostic)
fm.Underside: Complex pattern; small eyespots

desc: Unmistakable black with vivid red-orange forewing band. Males aggressively territorial.

fm.Upperside: Black with red-orange forewing band; red hindwing border; white apical spots
fm.Underside: Cryptic dark mottling

desc: Common buckeye — large eyespots on all four wings are the most prominent field mark of any LA butterfly. Open habitats. Uses plantain and snapdragon as hosts.

fm.Upperside: Brown; 2 forewing + 2 hindwing eyespots; orange bars
fm.Underside: Seasonally variable — tan/rose to deep brown

desc: Mourning cloak — among the longest-lived butterflies at 10-11 months. Dark maroon with pale yellow border. Overwinters as adult and flies on warm winter days.

fm.Upperside: Deep maroon with cream border and blue spot row
fm.Underside: Dark bark-like camouflage

desc: California tortoiseshell — medium orange-brown butterfly with dark borders. Famous for massive irruptive migrations in some years. Larvae on ceanothus.
Lorquin's admiral — stunning riparian butterfly with bold white median band and orange forewing tips. Graceful gliding flight along streams through willow corridors.

fm.Upperside: Black with white median band; orange forewing tips
fm.Underside: Orange-brown with white band; blue-gray margins

desc: California sister — LA's signature oak woodland butterfly. Bold white band and orange forewing patches over dark chocolate background. Graceful gliding flight through live oak canopy.

fm.Upperside: Dark with bold white band; large orange apex patch
fm.Underside: Complex blue, orange, cream, brown bands

desc: Mylitta crescent — small intricately patterned crescent butterfly. Low rapid flight through thistle patches. Multiple broods. Common in weedy habitats.

fm.Upperside: Orange with complex black reticulation
fm.Underside: Cream with tan patch and fine markings

desc: Common wood-nymph — large brown satyr with prominent yellow-ringed eyespots. Slow bobbing flight through grassy hillsides and meadow edges. Single summer brood.

fm.Upperside: Brown with 2 forewing eyespots in yellow patch
fm.Underside: Brown with eyespots and striping

desc: Fiery skipper — the most common lawn skipper in urban LA. Rapid darting flight low over irrigated turf. Males bright orange; females darker. Year-round.

fm.Upperside: Bright orange (♂) with jagged border; brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Orange with scattered dark spots (♂)

hp: Poaceae: Bermuda grass, crabgrass, lawn grasses

desc: Mournful duskywing — dark oak-associated skipper. Key field mark: white hindwing fringe (diagnostic vs. other duskywings). Common in oak woodland year-round.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown-black; conspicuous white hindwing fringe
fm.Underside: Dark with bold white fringe

hp: Fagaceae: Quercus agrifolia (coast live oak), interior live oak

desc: Funereal duskywing — dark skipper with narrower white hindwing fringe than the similar Mournful Duskywing. Legume-feeding. Common year-round in warm areas.

fm.Upperside: Dark; narrow white hindwing fringe
fm.Underside: Dark with thin white fringe

hp: Fabaceae: Acmispon glaber (deerweed), Olneya, other legumes

desc: Umber skipper — chunky dark skipper with warm umber-brown tones. On irrigated lawns and gardens. One of the few skippers comfortable in heavily urbanized areas.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with amber-orange spots
fm.Underside: Red-brown with paler spots

desc: Common checkered-skipper — small boldly checkered black-and-white skipper. Males blue-gray when fresh. Extremely common in disturbed habitats and gardens. Fast darting flight.

fm.Upperside: Black with white checkering; ♂ blue-gray hairs
fm.Underside: White with olive-tan bands

desc: Sandhill skipper — small tawny skipper with prominent veined hindwing underside pattern in LA populations. Grassy areas. Common in irrigated habitats.

fm.Upperside: Tawny (♂) with dark borders
fm.Underside: Yellow-green to olive with pale veining

desc: Eufala skipper — plain gray-brown skipper, often overlooked. Very common in irrigated landscapes. One of the most abundant but least noticed LA butterflies.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown; faint pale forewing spots
fm.Underside: Gray-brown with small pale hindwing spots

hp: Poaceae: various grasses inc. Bermuda, Johnson grass

desc: Northern white-skipper — bright white skipper with checkered fringes. Fast erratic flight. Arid hillsides and washes. Larvae on mallows. Desert-urban interface.

fm.Color: Bright white; checkered fringes; arid habitat

desc: LA's only metalmark. Intricate checkered pattern with metallic silver. Tied to Eriogonum.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown checkered with orange/white; metallic silver scales
fm.Underside: Orange with black-white checkering and silver spots

desc: Dark reddish-brown hairstreak of coniferous forests. San Gabriel Mtns above 5,000'.

fm.Upperside: Dark reddish-brown
fm.Underside: Reddish-brown; irregular white postmedian line

desc: Leda hairstreak — tiny blue-gray hairstreak with distinctive red-orange hindwing patches. Associated with mesquite. At desert-urban interface.

fm.Upperside: Blue-gray with orange marginal spots
fm.Underside: Gray with red-orange patches; white postmedian line

desc: Boisduval's blue — declining lupine-specialist blue butterfly. Once widespread, now restricted to montane sites where native lupines persist. Conservation concern.

fm.Upperside: Violet-blue (♂); dark brown with blue basal (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with black spots in white halos; no orange band

desc: Sylvan hairstreak — grayish lycaenid of riparian willows. Orange lunules on hindwing underside. Larvae on willows. Localized along riparian corridors.

fm.Color: Gray with orange HW lunules; on willows

desc: Two large (not 4–5 small) hindwing underside eyespots diagnostic. Cobwebby underwing.

fm.Upperside: Orange; small white spot in forewing cell
fm.Underside: Cobwebby pink-brown; 2 large hindwing eyespots
fm.Flight Period: Year-round; less common than V. cardui

desc: Great Basin wood-nymph — smaller and paler than Common Wood-Nymph. Dry grassy hillsides and chaparral edges. Slow bobbing flight. One or two eyespots on forewing.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown; 1–2 small eyespots; minimal yellow
fm.Underside: Gray-brown with striations

desc: Historical record only — formerly bred on yucca in Antelope Valley. Not documented in LA County since early 20th century. Extirpated from most of SoCal range.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown-black with yellow forewing spots
fm.Underside: Dark with frosted gray overscaling; yellow spots

desc: Quino checkerspot — FEDERALLY ENDANGERED butterfly. Orange-and-black checkerspot. Severely declining due to habitat loss. Once common in coastal sage scrub.
Common branded skipper — upland skipper with prominent silver-white chevron on hindwing underside. Mountain meadows and chaparral clearings. Late summer flier.

fm.Upperside: Orange (♂) with dark stigma; brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Olive/golden with silver-white chevron spots

desc: A coastal chaparral skipper similar to branded skipper but flying earlier in season.

fm.Upperside: Tawny orange (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Olive-green with pale elongated spots

desc: Desert black swallowtail — desert subspecies of black swallowtail. Black with yellow spots. Larvae on desert parsley. At desert-urban interface.
Large marble — larger than Sara Orangetip with bold green marbling on hindwing underside. In LA County restricted to San Gabriel Mountain peaks. Spring ephemeral.

fm.Upperside: White; bold black discal bar
fm.Underside: Bold green marbling

desc: Eastern tailed-blue — tiny blue butterfly with thin thread-like hindwing tails. At edge of western range in LA. Larvae on clovers and legumes. Very small.
Ceraunus blue — neotropical blue with single prominent round hindwing eyespot. Subtropical vagrant to LA. On legumes in weedy areas. Very small.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray; single large hindwing eyespot

desc: Reakirt's blue — small blue with prominent round spots in white halos on hindwing underside. Associated with mesquite and legumes. Desert and urban fringe.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Row of round black spots in white halos

desc: Satyr comma — the most common anglewing in LA County. Golden orange with ragged wing edges and a metallic silver comma mark on hindwing underside. On nettles.

fm.Upperside: Golden-orange; dark spots; ragged edges
fm.Underside: Brown bark-like; silver comma

desc: Zephyr anglewing — mountain anglewing of high elevations. San Gabriel Mountains above 6,000 feet. Ragged wing edges and silver 'comma' on hindwing underside.

fm.Upperside: Orange-brown; dark spots; ragged edges
fm.Underside: Dark; silver comma mark

desc: Texan crescent — neotropical crescent butterfly that appears as an irregular vagrant to LA. Dark with cream-white band. Normally found in Texas and Mexico.

fm.Upperside: Dark with white median band; red basal
fm.Underside: Brown with pale banding

desc: Woodland skipper — the last skipper still flying in autumn. Common in fall on garden flowers. Tawny orange. Marks the transition to winter.

fm.Upperside: Tawny-orange; dark borders
fm.Underside: Yellow-orange with pale spot-band

desc: Julia's skipper — tiny unmarked brown skipper easily overlooked in irrigated turf. One of the plainest butterflies in LA County. Common but rarely noticed.

fm.Upperside: Plain brown; faint pale spots
fm.Underside: Tan-brown; unmarked

desc: Columbian skipper — tawny skipper of foothill grasslands. Angular silver-white spots on greenish hindwing underside. Spring and fall broods.

fm.Upperside: Tawny-orange; dark stigma
fm.Underside: Olive-green with silver spots

desc: Propertius duskywing — gray-brown oak-feeding duskywing. Distinguished from Mournful by lack of white hindwing fringe. Chaparral and oak woodland.

fm.Upperside: Dark gray-brown; pale forewing spots; gray fringe
fm.Underside: Dark

desc: Pacuvius duskywing — dark brown skipper whose larvae feed on ceanothus. Chaparral and woodland habitats. Rapid darting flight. Important ceanothus pollinator.

fm.Color: Dark brown with subtle markings; on ceanothus

desc: Desert checkered-skipper — paler, more washed-out version of Common Checkered-Skipper. Desert habitats. More white, less bold pattern than its widespread relative.

fm.Upperside: Pale gray-white; sparse checkering
fm.Underside: Nearly white; olive bands

desc: Rural skipper — tawny orange skipper of woodland clearings and meadow edges. Larvae on grasses. Common in foothill oak woodland glades.
Small dark brown hairstreak of chaparral. Single spring brood. Perches with wings closed showing uniform brown underside.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown (rarely seen)
fm.Underside: Uniform warm brown; darker basal area

desc: A warm brown oak-feeding hairstreak. Named for Gold Rush era collectors. Orange submarginal spots below.

fm.Upperside: Brown with faint orange spots
fm.Underside: Gray-brown; orange submarginal crescents; dark postmedian spots

desc: Gray hairstreak associated with mountain mahogany. Thin tails. Montane chaparral.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown
fm.Underside: Pale gray with dark postmedian line; small orange cap near tail

desc: A reddish-brown hairstreak of mistletoe in conifers. Similar to Nelson's but warmer brown.

fm.Upperside: Brown
fm.Underside: Reddish-brown with white postmedian line bordered blue

desc: Juniper hairstreak — bright green underside like Bramble Hairstreak but on junipers rather than buckwheat. Desert edge and montane chaparral.

fm.Upperside: Brown
fm.Underside: Bright green with white postmedian line
fm.Habitat: Juniper woodland, desert edge with junipers

desc: Buckwheat-feeding green hairstreak. Smaller and duller than Bramble. Spring flier.

fm.Upperside: Brown
fm.Underside: Gray-green; white postmedian line sometimes reduced
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, chaparral with buckwheat

desc: Tiny blue tightly associated with Dudleya (liveforever succulents). One of earliest spring butterflies. Rocky coastal bluffs.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown with blue base (♀); orange HW spots
fm.Underside: White with black spots; orange HW band
fm.Habitat: Rocky coastal bluffs, cliff faces with Dudleya

desc: White arrowhead-shaped spots on hindwing underside diagnostic. Lupine specialist.

fm.Upperside: Blue-violet (♂); brown with blue base (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with white arrowhead-shaped HW spots

desc: Tiny pale blue of mesic habitats. Very small — among tiniest LA blues after Pygmy.

fm.Upperside: Pale blue (♂); gray (♀)
fm.Underside: Pale gray-white with scattered dark dots

desc: Orange-and-black checkerspot of mountain meadows. Distinguished from Mylitta by habitat and larger size.

fm.Upperside: Orange with complex black pattern and cream spots
fm.Underside: Cream and orange with dark-outlined cells

desc: Leanira checkerspot — boldly patterned black checkerspot with cream bands and orange patches. Multiple SoCal subspecies. Rocky chaparral with paintbrush host plants.

fm.Upperside: Black with cream/white bands; orange patches
fm.Underside: Cream with orange and black pattern

desc: Great Basin fritillary — large orange fritillary at range edge in the San Gabriel Mountains. Greenish underside with silver spots. Mountain meadows.

fm.Upperside: Tawny; dark spots
fm.Underside: Greenish with burnished silver spots

desc: A Batesian mimic of Pipevine Swallowtail. Blue-black with orange spots. Rare vagrant to desert edge LA.

fm.Upperside: Blue-black iridescence; no white band
fm.Underside: Dark with orange submarginal spots; blue marginal area
fm.Habitat: Riparian; rare vagrant to LA desert edge

desc: Subtropical vagrant. White/gray with orange FW patch. Reaches LA County very rarely from Mexico.

fm.Upperside: White-gray with orange forewing costal patch
fm.Underside: White with faint banding
fm.Flight: Year-round in tropics; vagrant to LA

desc: Large dark skipper. Desert canyons with Robinia (locust). Rare vagrant to LA from AZ.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with faint pale spots
fm.Underside: Dark with violet sheen

desc: Liban skipper — small dark checkered skipper restricted to desert saltbush flats. Specialist on Atriplex (saltbush). One of the most habitat-restricted LA skippers.

fm.Upperside: Black-brown with small white checkers
fm.Underside: Dark with scattered white spots

desc: Common sootywing — small dark skipper with white head spots. Associated with Amaranthaceae (pigweed, lamb's-quarters). Weedy urban lots. Easily overlooked.

fm.Upperside: Black-brown with fine white flecking; white head spots
fm.Underside: Black-brown with faint pale spots

desc: Sachem — large orange skipper. Males with large brown stigma on forewing. Females distinctively patterned with glassy forewing spots. Common on lawns and gardens.

fm.Upperside: ♂: bright orange with dark borders. ♀: dark with orange spots.
fm.Underside: ♂: orange-tan with large diffuse brown HW patch (diagnostic)

hp: Poaceae: Bermuda grass, crabgrass, Dallis grass

desc: Subspecies of Western Branded Skipper. Late-summer flier in SoCal mountains; grasses. Distinguished from other Hesperia by ventral hindwing pattern.

fm.Upperside: Orange-brown; dark margins
fm.Underside: Rust-orange with cream chevron spots

desc: Large skipper with prominent silver-white HW patch below. Strong rapid flight. Widespread but irregular in LA.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with gold FW spots; elongated HW
fm.Underside: Dark with large silver-white HW patch (diagnostic)
fm.Habitat: Woodland edges, gardens with legume vines

desc: Neotropical skipper with iridescent green thorax and long HW tails. Rare vagrant to SoCal.

fm.Upperside: Dark with iridescent blue-green thorax; long HW tails
fm.Underside: Brown with dark bands; long tails
fm.Flight: Year-round in tropics; vagrant to LA summer–fall

desc: Buckwheat specialist similar to Square-spotted. Dots not squares. Multiple local subspecies.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray; orange HW band with round (not square) spots
fm.Flight: Apr–Jul; single brood sync with Eriogonum
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage, chaparral slopes with buckwheat

desc: Western elfin — reddish-brown elfin of chaparral slopes. Jagged postmedian line on underside distinctive. On manzanita and ceanothus. Early spring flier.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown
fm.Underside: Rich reddish-brown with irregular/jagged pale postmedian line

desc: A blue of mountain meadows. Similar to Acmon but typically at higher elevation. Orange HW band usually narrower.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown with orange band (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray; orange HW band typically narrower than Acmon

desc: Western giant swallowtail — very large yellow-and-black swallowtail. Largest butterfly in LA County (5+ in wingspan). Larvae on citrus ('orange dogs'). Expanding range northward.

A large, showy checkerspot common in chaparral and coastal sage scrub. Highly variable — populations range from predominantly black to heavily orange/red. Larvae feed communally in silk webs on host plants. 1,863 LA County iNat obs

fm.Upperside: Black with red/orange/cream checkered spots; highly variable between populations
fm.Underside: Cream/yellow with red and black bands and spots
fm.Host Plants: Scrophulariaceae — Diplacus (monkeyflower), Castilleja, Penstemon; also Plantago
fm.Habitat: Chaparral, coastal sage scrub, oak woodland edges

hp: Scrophulariaceae — Diplacus (monkeyflower), Castilleja, Penstemon; also Plantago

desc: Recently split from Common Buckeye (J. coenia). The western/dry-habitat species — grayer overall, eyespots tend smaller. The dominant buckeye in arid LA County habitats.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown (less warm than J. coenia) with eyespots; eyespots may average smaller
fm.Underside: Variable — tan to rose-brown; often grayer than coenia
fm.Habitat: Drier, more arid habitats than J. coenia — desert edges, dry washes, chaparral trails

desc: Most common small blue in LA chaparral. Orange HW marginal band with metallic green caps diagnostic. Genus recently moved from Plebejus to Icaricia on iNat. 1,482 LA County obs

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂) or brown with orange marginal band (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with black spots; vivid orange HW band with metallic green caps
fm.Habitat: Chaparral, coastal sage scrub, rocky hillsides, trails

desc: LA County's second metalmark (alongside Mormon). Smaller, darker, more common in warm desert-edge habitats. Tight buckwheat association.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown-black with white spots in regular rows; metallic scales
fm.Underside: Gray-brown with white spots and metallic silver scaling
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, desert scrub, chaparral — warm rocky slopes with buckwheat

desc: The correct iNat-current name for the white checkered-skipper in SoCal. Paler than Common Checkered-Skipper. 702 LA County obs

fm.Upperside: Pale gray-white with sparse dark checkering; ♂ blue-gray hairs fresh
fm.Underside: Nearly white with faint olive-tan bands
fm.Habitat: Desert washes, arid scrub, open disturbed ground

desc: A buckwheat-specialist blue. Distinguished from Square-spotted Blue by range and subtle underside pattern differences.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with black spots; orange HW marginal band
fm.Flight Period: May–Sep; single brood, sync with buckwheat bloom
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, chaparral slopes with buckwheat

desc: LA County's third metalmark — associated with Baccharis in riparian and desert wash habitats. "Fatal" name from original description, not toxicity.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with darker median bands; metallic silver-gray spots
fm.Underside: Orange-brown with metallic silver spots
fm.Flight Period: Year-round in warm areas; multiple broods
fm.Habitat: Desert washes, riparian edges with Baccharis

desc: Federally Endangered. Restricted to remnant coastal dune habitat at LAX and nearby sites. One of the rarest butterflies in North America. iNat name differs from our previous entry — updating. (mostly from managed habitat).

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with prominent black spots; orange marginal band
fm.Flight Period: Jun–Aug; single brood, sync with coast buckwheat
fm.Habitat: Coastal dune scrub — El Segundo dunes, LAX area

desc: Federally Endangered. Once thought extinct, rediscovered 1994. Restricted to coastal sage scrub on Palos Verdes Peninsula — one of the rarest butterflies in the world. Captive breeding program ongoing. LA County endemic.

fm.Upperside: ♂: silvery-blue with narrow black border. ♀: brownish-gray.
fm.Underside: Gray with dark spots ringed in white
fm.Host Plants: Astragalus trichopodus var. lonchus, Acmispon glaber (deerweed)
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub — Palos Verdes Peninsula only

hp: Astragalus trichopodus var. lonchus, Acmispon glaber (deerweed)

desc: Orange-and-black checkerspot of chaparral. Named for William Gabb, California geological surveyor. Closely tied to Hazardia squarrosa host plant.

fm.Upperside: Orange with black markings; cream spots near margins
fm.Underside: Cream/yellow with orange and black patterning
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, chaparral, rocky hillsides

desc: iNat-current name for the California population (split from C. tullia). Small bouncy satyr of grasslands and mountain meadows.

fm.Upperside: Plain tawny-orange
fm.Underside: Olive-tan with small silver-pupiled eyespot; gray hindwing

desc: A coastal salt marsh specialist — one of the most habitat-restricted skippers in California. Brown with faint pale spots. Strong flier that wanders from marsh habitat.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with faint pale forewing spots
fm.Underside: Brown with faint purplish sheen; pale vein streaks
fm.Habitat: Coastal salt marshes — Ballona Wetlands, Malibu Lagoon, other remnant marshes

desc: A Neotropical sulphur reaching LA County. Larger and more orange than Cloudless Sulphur. Irregular vagrant/colonizer.

fm.Upperside: ♂: deep orange with subtle darker margins. ♀: paler, yellow-orange.
fm.Underside: Yellow-orange with small reddish cell spots
fm.Habitat: Gardens, open areas during immigration years

desc: iNat-current genus (Tharsalea, moved from Lycaena). Copper-orange with delicate hindwing tail. Associated with Ribes in mountain chaparral.

fm.Upperside: ♂: bright copper-orange. ♀: brown with orange highlights.
fm.Underside: Gray-white with black spots; thin hindwing tail
fm.Habitat: Mountain chaparral, oak woodland with Ribes understory

desc: Catalina Island endemic — one of the most geographically restricted butterflies in North America. Gray with blue hindwing scaling. Deerweed specialist. (mostly Catalina).

fm.Upperside: Gray with blue-purple tint, especially ♂
fm.Underside: Pale gray; dark postmedian line; small orange/black HW spots near tail
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub on Santa Catalina Island (LA County)

desc: iNat-current name (moved from Abaeis). Bright orange Pierid of warm lowlands. "Sleepy" from closed-eye forewing mark.

fm.Upperside: Bright orange with irregular black borders
fm.Underside: Seasonally variable — yellow (summer) to brick-red (winter)
fm.Habitat: Desert washes, warm open areas, urban edges

desc: A large, boldly marked white of desert-edge habitats. Green marbling on HW underside heavier than Checkered White. Desert-adapted.

fm.Upperside: White with heavy dark markings especially in ♀; greenish tint fresh
fm.Underside: HW heavily marbled with yellow-green (bolder than Checkered White)
fm.Habitat: Desert washes, alkaline flats, desert-edge with Brassicaceae

desc: Gorgon copper — large copper with intricate spotted underside on buckwheat. Males with purple-gray upperside. Chaparral hillsides. Spring flier.

fm.Upperside: ♂: bright copper with dark spots. ♀: brown-orange with heavy spotting.
fm.Underside: Gray-tan with black spots; orange HW marginal band
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, chaparral with buckwheat

desc: A mountain crescent similar to Mylitta but typically at higher elevations with Aster host plants (not thistles).

fm.Upperside: Orange with black reticulation; broader dark markings than Mylitta
fm.Underside: Cream/yellow with brown patch; pattern more contrasting than Mylitta

desc: iNat-current genus. High San Gabriel Mountains endemic — one of LA County's rarest residents. Associated with grape soda lupine above 7,000'.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray with dark spots; veined appearance
fm.Habitat: High San Gabriel Mtns above 7,000' — extremely restricted

desc: The largest NA copper — almost hairstreak-sized. Distinctive pale underside with large black spots. Dock specialist.

fm.Upperside: ♂: gray with orange submarginal spots. ♀: orange with dark spots.
fm.Underside: White/cream with bold round black spots; orange submarginal HW band
fm.Habitat: Moist meadows, streambanks, agricultural edges with dock

desc: Desert/sagebrush checkerspot. Orange and black with cream spots. Desert edge and Mojave.

fm.Upperside: Orange with black markings; cream marginal spots
fm.Underside: Cream with orange bands and black outlines

desc: iNat-current genus. Higher elevation than Acmon with broader orange band. Lupine specialist.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown with broader orange band (♀)
fm.Underside: Like Acmon but orange band broader

desc: A mountain grassland skipper with prominent chevron spots on greenish underside.

fm.Upperside: Tawny-orange (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Olive-green with pale chevron spot-band

desc: A desert spring ephemeral. Vivid orange tips like Sara Orangetip but with more yellow-tinted underside.

fm.Upperside: White/yellow with orange FW tips (♂); reduced (♀)
fm.Underside: HW marbled green on yellow ground (more yellow than Sara)
fm.Habitat: Desert washes, desert scrub, Joshua tree woodland

desc: Callippe fritillary — large orange fritillary with silver spots on hindwing underside. Montane meadows in San Gabriel Mountains. At range edge. Spring-summer flier.

fm.Upperside: Tawny orange; black spots and bars
fm.Underside: Olive-brown with smaller silver spots

desc: Unusual hairstreak — golden-brown rather than gray. Associated with canyon live oak at mid-elevations. Single-brooded.

fm.Upperside: Golden-brown; smooth unmarked
fm.Underside: Golden-tan with faint darker postmedian line; no tails
fm.Habitat: Canyon live oak woodland, rocky canyon slopes

desc: Desert buckwheat specialist. Distinguished from Bernardino by range (Mojave side) and minor pattern differences.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Gray; orange HW band; spots slightly different from bernardino

desc: iNat-current genus. Large orange fritillary with silver spots. Mountain meadows.

fm.Upperside: Orange with dark spots; black veins
fm.Underside: HW with prominent silver spots on greenish-brown ground

desc: Unmistakable — elongated palps form a "snout." Orange-and-brown pattern. Hackberry specialist. Rare in LA County — mostly desert edge.

fm.Upperside: Brown with orange patches; elongated snout-like palps (diagnostic)
fm.Underside: Gray-brown; leaf-like camouflage when closed
fm.Flight Period: Year-round in tropics; vagrant to LA

desc: Neotropical. Long narrow wings with bold black and yellow stripes. Communal rooster. Occasional vagrant to SoCal — may establish in warm years with Passiflora.

fm.Upperside: Black with bold yellow stripes
fm.Underside: Similar with red basal spots
fm.Flight Period: Year-round in tropics; vagrant to LA summer–fall
fm.Habitat: Gardens with passionvine during vagrant events

desc: A small gray-green hairstreak of desert-edge habitats with antelope brush. Rare at western range edge.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown
fm.Underside: Gray-green with thin white postmedian line; small orange spot near tail

desc: Juba skipper — montane branded skipper with jagged and angular greenish spots on hindwing underside. Mountain meadows and chaparral clearings. Strong fast flight.

fm.Upperside: Tawny-orange (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Olive-green with large jagged/angular white spots

desc: Harford's sulphur — medium yellow butterfly of foothill chaparral. California endemic. On deer weed and other native legumes. Uncommon — declining.
Desert spring white. Bold green marbling on underside. Very early spring flier in desert canyons.

fm.Upperside: White; black discal spot on FW
fm.Underside: HW with bold irregular green marble pattern on white

desc: Pallid blue — extremely pale buckwheat specialist. One of the most localized butterflies in LA County. Desert buckwheat flats. Distinguished by very faded wing pattern.

fm.Upperside: Pale blue (♂); pale brown (♀)
fm.Underside: Very pale gray; reduced spotting

desc: A dark, robust spread-wing skipper. White FW spots aligned in an arc. Very rare in LA.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown with aligned white FW spots in an arc
fm.Underside: Dark brown with faint pale bands

desc: Old World citrus pest swallowtail — NOT native. Three LA records may represent accidental importation on citrus shipments or deliberate releases. Invasive threat if established.

fm.Upperside: Black/brown with scattered yellow spots (not banded like native swallowtails)
fm.Underside: Yellow with blue and red HW spots
fm.Host Plants: Rutaceae — Citrus (major pest in Old World)

hp: Rutaceae — Citrus (major pest in Old World)

desc: Southern dogface — yellow sulphur with dark forewing 'dog face' pattern. Similar to California Dogface but smaller and less vivid. Desert-edge. On indigo bush.

fm.Upperside: ♂: yellow with dark "dogface" outline. ♀: yellow-green.
fm.Underside: Greenish-yellow; pink cell spot

desc: Alkali skipper — tiny pale skipper restricted to alkaline desert flats. One of the most habitat-specialized butterflies. Saltgrass dependent. Extremely localized.

fm.Upperside: Pale orange-yellow; narrow dark borders
fm.Underside: Pale yellow; unmarked or faint veining

desc: Desert checkerspot. Bold orange/cream/black pattern. — extreme vagrant from desert.

fm.Upperside: Bold orange and cream patches on black ground
fm.Underside: Cream with orange and black pattern
fm.Habitat: Sonoran desert scrub; vagrant to LA basin

desc: A tailless Neotropical swallowtail. Gold-edged black wings. Extremely rare vagrant to LA.

fm.Upperside: Black with gold/yellow submarginal band; no tails
fm.Underside: Black with red basal spots; gold band

desc: Moss's elfin — small reddish-brown elfin of rocky habitats with stonecrop (Dudleya). Very localized — found only where host plant grows on rocky outcrops.

fm.Upperside: Brown
fm.Underside: Reddish-brown basally; frosted gray outwardly; jagged line between

desc: Mallow scrub-hairstreak — subtropical hairstreak reaching its northern range limit in LA. Associated with mallows. Rare — among the most infrequently recorded hairstreaks.

fm.Upperside: Gray-brown
fm.Underside: Pale gray; dark postmedian line; small spots

desc: Silver-banded hairstreak — tiny tropical hairstreak with brilliant metallic green underside crossed by a silver band. Subtropical vagrant. On balloon vine.

fm.Upperside: Dark with purple sheen (♂)
fm.Underside: Brilliant green; silver-white postmedian band

desc: Yucca giant-skipper — large dark skipper whose larvae bore inside living yucca stalks, creating silk-lined tunnels. Adults rapid and powerful. Desert margins.

fm.Upperside: Dark brown-black with yellow FW spots and narrow yellow HW border
fm.Underside: Dark with frosted gray overscaling
fm.Host Plants: Yucca spp. — larvae bore in living root crown
fm.Habitat: Desert yucca grasslands, Joshua tree woodland

hp: Yucca spp. — larvae bore in living root crown

desc: Recently described/split species. Similar to Woodland Skipper. Taxonomy still being resolved.

fm.Upperside: Tawny-orange
fm.Underside: Pale yellow-orange with spot-band

desc: Tropical nymphalid with pale white wings and orange margins. Single LA County obs— extreme vagrant. Establishing in south Florida/Texas.

fm.Upperside: White with small brown spots; orange HW margin and eyespot
fm.Underside: Similar but paler
fm.Habitat: Tropical wetlands; extreme vagrant to LA

desc: Famous Monarch mimic. Not a regular LA County resident — single obslikely a stray. Distinguished from Monarch by black postmedian HW line.

fm.Upperside: Orange with black veins (like Monarch) PLUS black postmedian HW line (diagnostic)
fm.Underside: Like Monarch but with black HW crossline
fm.Habitat: Riparian woodland; extreme vagrant to LA

desc: Orange-barred sulphur — large bright orange neotropical sulphur. Only a single LA County observation — extreme vagrant from tropical Mexico. Spectacular if seen.

fm.Upperside: ♂: bright orange with orange bar. ♀: yellow with pink-orange patches.
fm.Underside: Yellow with pink/red cell spots

desc: San Emigdio blue — desert-edge alkali specialist found at scattered sites with saltgrass. Small pale blue. Named for Fort San Emigdio area. Very localized.

fm.Upperside: Blue (♂); brown (♀)
fm.Underside: White with black dots; no orange band

desc: Orange skipperling — one of the tiniest skippers, entirely unmarked orange above. Rapid low flight through grass. Desert margins and foothill meadows.

fm.Upperside: Bright unmarked orange
fm.Underside: Orange, unmarked

desc: Black swallowtail butterfly — black with yellow spot rows. Blue HW wash (♀). Larvae on carrot family (parsley, dill, fennel). Occasional in LA — at edge of western range.

fm.Color: Black with yellow spots; ♀ blue HW wash

desc: Sleepy duskywing — small dark skipper butterfly. Larvae on scrub oaks. Early spring flier in chaparral. Wings held flat at rest (unlike most butterflies).
